Defines global built-in objects, values, and routines for the ECMA-262 standard in JerryScript.

# Purpose
The code defines a set of macros and properties for global built-in objects and functions in compliance with the ECMAScript Language Specification, specifically ECMA-262 version 5. It provides a structured way to define identifiers, simple values, number values, object values, and routine properties for the global object. The code uses macros such as `OBJECT_ID`, `SIMPLE_VALUE`, `NUMBER_VALUE`, `OBJECT_VALUE`, and `ROUTINE` to declare various properties and methods associated with the global object, including constants like `undefined`, `NaN`, and `Infinity`, as well as built-in objects like `Object`, `Function`, `Array`, `String`, and error types.

The file also includes conditional compilation directives to enable or disable certain built-in objects and functions based on configuration flags. For example, it conditionally includes built-in objects like `Array`, `String`, `Boolean`, `Number`, `Date`, `RegExp`, `Math`, and `JSON`, as well as error types like `EvalError`, `RangeError`, `ReferenceError`, `SyntaxError`, `TypeError`, and `URIError`. Additionally, it defines routines such as `eval`, `parseFloat`, `isNaN`, `isFinite`, and URI encoding/decoding functions. The use of macros and configuration flags allows for flexible inclusion of features based on specific requirements or constraints.
